title: campanula × murithiana
intro: Campanula × murithiana, also known as the Alpine Harebell, is a hybrid plant species within the Campanula genus. This plant is known for its blue or white flowers and is used in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk remedies. Campanula × murithiana contains compounds with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. It is often prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
